channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Correction to: The Changing Nature of Homicide in the Twin Cities: An Interrupted Time Series Analysis of the Police Murder of George Floyd
item.description:
Authors: Larson et al.
Journal: Journal of Quantitative Criminology

item.title: The Impact of Occupational Suffering on Military Police Officers in a Public Security Crisis Region: A Psychodynamic Approach to Work
item.description:
Authors: Dantas Motta et al.
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

Abstract This study investigates how working conditions impact the physical and psychological suffering of Military Police officers in Baixada Fluminense, Rio de Janeiro, focusing on the role of occupational suffering in mental health distress. A quantitative field study was conducted with 61 active Military Police officers using a questionnaire based on Christophe Dejours’s Psychodynamics of Work theory. Data were analyzed using descriptive and...
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: The Impact of Prior Military Experience and Burnout on Police Officer Use of Force
item.description:
Authors: Fiske & Schriver
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Cumulative Exposure to Police-Involved Shootings and Mental Health Conditions in Minneapolis, Minnesota
item.description:
Authors: Tanveer et al.
Journal: Journal of Urban Health

item.title: Police body-worn cameras and fatal police shootings: A national quasi-experimental study of aggregate effects and treatment effect heterogeneity
item.description:
Authors: Sheppard, Keller G.
Journal: Journal of Criminal Justice

item.title: “They need to be taught a lesson”: police officer rationalisations in dismissal reviews
item.description:
Authors: Tsahuridu et al.
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose A dismissed Victoria Police officer or protective services officer has the option to seek a review by the Police Registration and Services Board. The Board must then review to affirm the decision to dismiss unless it is satisfied that the Victoria Police decision to do so was “harsh, unjust or unreasonable”. This research analyses 59 Review Decisions made by the Board between 2014 and 2024. ...
